tool.name: AgrVATE
tool.description: typing-only Staphylococcus aureus agr locus classification
tool.command: #if $input_fasta.is_of_type('fasta.gz'): gunzip -c '$input_fasta' > input.fasta && #else: ln -sf '$input_fasta' input.fasta && #end if agrvate -i input.fasta --typing-only --force && cp 'input-results/input-summary.tab' '$summary' && cp 'input-results/input-agr_gp.tab' '$agr_group_hits'

param.name: input_fasta
param.label: S. aureus genome assembly FASTA
param.help: Input genome assembly in FASTA format (gzip allowed). AgrVATE is intended for Staphylococcus aureus assemblies.

tool.help:
AgrVATE: Agr Variant Assessment & Typing Engine ================================================
AgrVATE identifies the Staphylococcus aureus agr locus type from a genome assembly FASTA file.
This Galaxy wrapper runs AgrVATE in typing-only mode::
agrvate -i input.fasta --typing-only --force
Input -----
- A Staphylococcus aureus genome assembly in FASTA format (gzip accepted).
Outputs -------
- **agr typing summary** — one row per sample with the agr group call (gp1–gp4, ``u`` for unknown, ``m`` for multiple) and a per-step pass/fail breakdown across input check, databases check, agr typing, operon check and snippy frameshift detection. - **agr group hits** — the kmer-search table that produced the call.
Database --------
The bioconda ``agrvate`` package ships its database directory at::
$CONDA_PREFIX/share/agrvate-<version>/agrvate_databases/
AgrVATE auto-discovers this path when installed via conda, so this wrapper does not expose the ``-d`` / ``--databases`` option and does not require a Galaxy Data Manager.
Notes -----
This wrapper intentionally uses AgrVATE typing-only mode. It does not run the optional MUMmer / Snippy-based agr operon extraction or frameshift detection steps. If you need those, run AgrVATE through the bactopia ``agrvate`` workflow or extend this wrapper to expose the ``-m`` flag and the additional output files (``*-agr_operon.fna``, ``*-snippy/``).
